key_insight: DeFAb shows that defeasible abduction can be benchmarked with fully verifiable logic, revealing large gaps between symbolic solvers and frontier language models.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark provides a rigorous testbed for trustworthy reasoning systems and a verifier that can also serve as an exact reward signal for preference optimization.
method: Builds instances from 18 public knowledge sources, combining taxonomies and property graphs.
Generates 372,648+ defeasible abduction problems with polynomial-time checks for derivation, conservativity, and minimality.
Adds harder variants, including DeFAb-Hard and CONJURE, to test robustness and creativity.
evidence_signal: Across 33.75M materialized rules, a rule-based solver achieves 100% accuracy in under 50 microseconds per instance, while frontier models peak at 65% and drop to 23.5% under rendering-robust evaluation.
watch_item: Performance is highly sensitive to surface rendering and evaluation setup, so benchmark results may not transfer cleanly to real-world open-ended reasoning tasks.
paper_title: DeFAb: A Verifiable Benchmark for Defeasible Abduction in Foundation Models
